tasks.title: Extract comparison/history workspace panel ownership out of App.tsx
tasks.description: Completed the first App.tsx decomposition stage by moving comparison/history workspace panel ownership into `runHistoryWorkspacePanels.tsx`, keeping `App.tsx` focused on route composition and handler wiring while adding targeted workspace-panel tests.
tasks.direction_relation_note: The latest main-session reply confirms this stage landed in commit `756a6b9` with `runHistoryWorkspacePanels.tsx` and matching tests, satisfying the workspace-level ownership extraction that was previously at the queue head.
tasks.priority_reason: Marked done because the main session completed, validated, committed, and pushed the workspace-panel ownership split; the remaining App.tsx pressure moved to `RunSection` and comparison/history browser internals.

tasks.title: Extract RunSection and comparison/history browser implementation out of App.tsx
tasks.description: Completed the App.tsx decomposition stage by moving `RunSection` and the comparison/history browser implementation into `features/run-history/RunSection.tsx`, leaving `App.tsx` as route wiring plus imports while preserving the formatter contract and route-level tests.
tasks.direction_relation_note: The latest main-session reply confirms this extraction landed in commit `aa3f740`, with `RunSection.tsx` now owning the browser implementation and the route smoke/typecheck coverage passing, so this stage is complete.
tasks.priority_reason: Marked done because the main session finished the RunSection/browser extraction, validated it with `npm run typecheck` and focused tests, and pushed the result to `origin/main`.

tasks.title: Move shared run-history tooltip formatters out of RunSection.tsx
tasks.description: Completed the formatter-boundary cleanup by moving the shared comparison tooltip formatter helpers into `features/comparisonTooltipFormatters.ts`, rewiring `RunSection.tsx`, `App.tsx`, and the query-builder relative-time path to consume the shared module without behavior changes.
tasks.direction_relation_note: The latest main-session reply confirms this extraction landed in commit `9bc2ada`, with `comparisonTooltipFormatters.ts` becoming the neutral shared boundary and the duplicate query-builder relative-time implementation folded into the shared formatter path.
tasks.priority_reason: Marked done because the main session completed the formatter extraction, validated it with `cd apps/web && npm run typecheck` plus focused run-history/workspace tests, and pushed the result to `origin/main`.

tasks.title: Finish consolidating comparison tooltip helpers into the shared formatter layer
tasks.description: Completed the remaining comparison tooltip helper consolidation by moving the session summary/metadata, timestamp parsing, refresh interval, hash, tuning delta, preset conflict summary, import feedback, and relative timestamp label helpers into `features/comparisonTooltipFormatters.ts`, leaving `RunSection.tsx` with only UI and DOM interaction concerns.
tasks.direction_relation_note: The latest main-session reply confirms this final formatter/helper pass landed in commit `7d7f409`, and explicitly states there is no remaining formatter-layer consolidation work left in `RunSection.tsx` beyond UI/DOM helpers.
tasks.priority_reason: Marked done because the main session completed the remaining helper consolidation, validated it with `npm run typecheck` plus focused formatter/run-history tests, and pushed the result to `origin/main`.

tasks.title: Move guarded-live and provider-governance orchestration out of application.py
tasks.description: Completed the orchestration extraction by moving guarded-live worker session maintenance and provider-governance search-moderation catalog flows out of `application.py` into bounded `application_support` orchestration modules while keeping `TradingApplication` as a delegation facade.
tasks.direction_relation_note: The latest main-session reply confirms guarded-live orchestration now lives in `guarded_live_orchestration.py` and provider-governance orchestration now lives in `provider_governance_orchestration.py`, satisfying this compatibility-facade extraction milestone.
tasks.priority_reason: Marked done because the main session completed the orchestration move, validated it with `py_compile` plus focused pytest coverage, and pushed commit `0542476` to `origin/main`.

tasks.title: Extract remaining governance serializers and guarded-live helpers out of application.py
tasks.description: Continue the `application.py` compatibility-facade shrink by moving the remaining governance serializer/save/load/private helper paths and guarded-live primitive helpers into bounded `application_support` or service modules that the new orchestration layer can call directly.
tasks.direction_relation_note: The latest main-session reply explicitly says orchestration extraction is finished and names the next decomposition target as the governance serializer/save/load/private helpers plus guarded-live primitives still living in `application.py` around the new support-layer entrypoints.
tasks.priority_reason: Promoted to the sole ready queue head because the operator asked to continue with one queued task, the prior ready task is now complete, and this is the most direct executable continuation called out by the latest main-session `Remaining` section.

tasks.title: Split operator and guarded-live model families out of domain/models.py
tasks.description: Continue the `domain/models.py` compatibility re-export pattern by extracting the remaining operator and guarded-live model families into `domain/model_types/*` modules so new backend feature work no longer defaults to the broad shared model file.
tasks.direction_relation_note: The direction detail and current-state docs explicitly note that provider-provenance records already moved behind domain/models.py compatibility imports, while the remaining operator and guarded-live families still need the same bounded-family treatment.
tasks.priority_reason: This remains executable but stays proposed because the latest main-session reply points to finishing the remaining `application.py` support/helper decomposition first, so opening a second backend compatibility split now would dilute the immediate continuation.
